title: digitalis mariana
intro: Digitalis mariana, also known as the purple foxglove or island foxglove, is a biennial flowering plant native to the Channel Island of Jersey. The plant contains glycosides, specifically digoxin and digitoxin, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including treating heart conditions such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. The health benefits of Digitalis mariana include improved heart function, reduced symptoms of heart failure, and lower blood pressure. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal tincture, tea, or dried extract, although it should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to its potential toxicity.
